description: Core utilities layer: data profiling, statistical analysis, clustering, regression, time series forecasting, AI intelligence helpers, and enhanced data cleaning operations. This creates all the business-logic modules that the UI pages will consume.
steps: Step 1 - Update requirements.txt: Add fpdf2 to the existing requirements (scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels, python-docx are already present).

Step 2 - Create utils/profiling.py: (a) compute_quality_score(df) returns a 0-100 score computed from null_rate, duplicate_rate, type_consistency, and outlier_rate. (b) generate_column_profiles(df) returns a list of dicts with dtype, null_pct, unique_count, top_values (top 5 value_counts), and a mini-distribution (histogram bin edges/counts for numeric, value_counts for categorical). (c) generate_correlation_heatmap_data(df) returns the correlation matrix plus a list of pairs with abs(r)>0.85 flagged as redundant. (d) ai_profiler_commentary(df, api_key) calls Gemini via utils/ai_client.generate_content with a prompt that includes the quality score and column profiles, asks for a 2-3 sentence assessment plus top 3 cleaning recommendations. Wrap in try/except, return (text, error).

Step 3 - Create utils/statistics.py: (a) run_ttest(df, col, group_col, group_a, group_b) performs scipy.stats.ttest_ind, returns dict with t_stat, p_value, verdict (plain English). (b) run_chi_square(df, col_a, col_b) performs scipy.stats.chi2_contingency, returns dict with chi2, p_value, dof, verdict. (c) run_anova(df, value_col, group_col) performs scipy.stats.f_oneway across groups, returns dict with f_stat, p_value, verdict. (d) run_linear_regression(df, x_cols, y_col) uses sklearn LinearRegression, returns coefficients table (DataFrame), r_squared, and predictions for scatter overlay. (e) run_logistic_regression(df, x_cols, y_col) uses sklearn LogisticRegression (with label encoding), returns coefficients table, accuracy, classification_report_text. (f) run_kmeans_clustering(df, columns, max_k=10) runs elbow method (inertias for k=2..max_k), picks optimal k, returns cluster_labels, inertia_values, per_cluster_stats (mean per column per cluster). (g) ai_describe_clusters(cluster_stats, api_key) calls Gemini to describe each cluster in plain English.

Step 4 - Create utils/forecasting.py: (a) run_time_series_forecast(df, date_col, value_col, periods=12, freq='MS') uses statsmodels ExponentialSmoothing (Holt-Winters). Returns dict with actuals_df, forecast_df (with yhat, yhat_lower, yhat_upper confidence bands), and combined_df for plotting. Wrap model fitting in try/except with a fallback to simple exponential smoothing if seasonal model fails. (b) build_forecast_chart(result_dict) returns a Plotly figure with actuals as solid line, forecast as dashed, and confidence band as filled area.

Step 5 - Create utils/ai_intelligence.py: (a) generate_insight_cards(analysis_context, api_key) calls Gemini asking for structured JSON with keys: findings (list), risks (list), opportunities (list), recommendations (list). Parse the JSON response, fallback to empty lists. (b) explain_this_number(value, column_name, df, api_key) calls Gemini with context about the column stats and asks it to explain what the value means, what drives it, and how it compares. Returns (text, error). (c) generate_executive_summary_brief(df, api_key) produces a 400-word formal brief: Situation, Key Metrics, Findings, Risks, Recommendations. Returns (text, error).

Step 6 - Expand utils/data_engine.py: Add these new cleaning operations: (a) flag_missing(df, column) adds a boolean column '{column}_is_missing' and returns (df, count_flagged). (b) cap_outliers(df, column, multiplier=1.5) clips values to IQR bounds instead of removing rows, returns (df, count_capped). (c) split_column(df, column, delimiter, new_col_names) splits a text column into multiple new columns. (d) merge_columns(df, columns, separator, new_col_name) concatenates text columns. (e) standardize_text(df, column, mode='lowercase') applies lowercase/uppercase/title/strip to a text column. (f) convert_column_type(df, column, target_type) converts column dtype (numeric, datetime, string, category).

Step 7 - Create utils/dashboard_builder.py: (a) detect_kpi_columns(df) heuristically picks top numeric columns suitable for KPI cards (non-ID, reasonable range). (b) build_kpi_card_data(df, col, date_col=None) computes current value, delta vs previous period (if date_col), and sparkline data (last N periods). (c) auto_build_dashboard(df, api_key=None) analyzes the schema (column types, relationships) and returns a list of chart specs [{chart_type, x, y, color, title}] for the 4-6 most relevant charts. If api_key provided, use Gemini to pick charts; otherwise use rule-based heuristic. (d) CHART_REGISTRY dict mapping chart_type names to Plotly figure builder functions (bar, line, area, scatter, pie, donut, box, histogram, waterfall, gauge, treemap).

Step 8 - Create utils/sensitivity.py: (a) detect_sensitive_columns(df) scans column names and sample values for patterns indicating personal data (email regex, phone regex, SSN patterns, columns named 'email', 'phone', 'ssn', 'id_number', etc.). Returns a list of (column_name, reason) tuples. (b) mask_column(df, column) replaces values with masked versions (e.g. 'j***@example.com' for emails, '***-***-1234' for phones).
acceptance_criteria: python -m py_compile utils/data_engine.py passes with the new functions

All new util modules can be imported without error: python -c 'from utils.profiling import compute_quality_score; from utils.statistics import run_ttest; from utils.forecasting import run_time_series_forecast; from utils.ai_intelligence import generate_insight_cards; from utils.dashboard_builder import auto_build_dashboard; from utils.sensitivity import detect_sensitive_columns; print("OK")'
New functions in data_engine.py are importable: python -c 'from utils.data_engine import flag_missing, cap_outliers, split_column, merge_columns, standardize_text, convert_column_type; print("OK")'
verification: python -m py_compile utils/profiling.py && python -m py_compile utils/statistics.py && python -m py_compile utils/forecasting.py && python -m py_compile utils/ai_intelligence.py && python -m py_compile utils/dashboard_builder.py && python -m py_compile utils/sensitivity.py && python -m py_compile utils/data_engine.py

python -c "from utils.profiling import compute_quality_score, generate_column_profiles, generate_correlation_heatmap_data; from utils.statistics import run_ttest, run_chi_square, run_anova, run_linear_regression, run_logistic_regression, run_kmeans_clustering; from utils.forecasting import run_time_series_forecast, build_forecast_chart; from utils.ai_intelligence import generate_insight_cards, explain_this_number, generate_executive_summary_brief; from utils.dashboard_builder import auto_build_dashboard, detect_kpi_columns, CHART_REGISTRY; from utils.sensitivity import detect_sensitive_columns, mask_column; from utils.data_engine import flag_missing, cap_outliers, split_column, merge_columns, standardize_text, convert_column_type; print('All new utils import OK')"
